content: Hi all! I was trying to get started with Rust for Linux stuff and then got pulled away for a while. I'm back trying to build the kernel from source and mainly wanted to check in around mailing list stuff. I've never contributed to a kernel mailing list before but know they get pretty angry about non-plaintext messages so I always assumed the Gmail web interface was a non-starter. I see that Gmail recently added a legitimate plaintext mode as far as I can tell (text/plain content type). Does this invalidate the kernel's previous documentation saying "Don't use Gmail's web interface" or are there additional problems even using that mode?
Gmail's plain text mode is fine for normal messages, but it does not work for patches themselves because Gmail will modify the contents (e.g. by wrapping the lines).
IIRC some years ago there was a way to avoid the wrapping even from the web interface (I think I sent a patch that way), but nowadays I am not aware of any way of avoiding it.
So I recommend you either use a client like Mutt which also easily allows you to import patches as emails from e.g. git format-patch, or using git send-email.
Having said that, for the moment, if it is for Rust for Linux, you can use GitHub's PRs until we get merged.
Wonderful! I was hoping PRs would be workable. Good to know about line wrapping. I need to send some other patches to the list for a documentation bug anyway.
John Baublitz has marked this topic as resolved.
